title: convolvulus mollissimus
intro: Convolvulus mollissimus, also known as the hairy bindweed or soft hedge bindweed, is a perennial vine native to Australia and New Zealand, characterized by its hairy stems and trumpet-shaped flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Convolvulus mollissimus is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a medical professional.
